title: AccessRequest
description: The persisted form of a principal's request for elevated capability on a workflow (design §16.5): the target workflow, the requested capability scopes, the requesting subject the grant will key on, plus the decision state and audit/optimistic-concurrency metadata. Backends store this document verbatim.

baseWorkflowId.description: The base workflow id the request targets; approval routes to this workflow's §15 administrators.

requestedScopes.description: The capability scopes (design §14.1) requested, e.g. runs:write. An approval grants at most these, capped by the platform allowlist.

subjectClaimType.description: The principal claim type the granted entitlement will key on (e.g. 'sub') — captured from the requester so the grant is scoped to exactly them.

subjectClaimValue.description: The requester's value for subjectClaimType (e.g. their subject id).

requesterLabel.description: An optional human-friendly label for the requester (e.g. a username), for display only.

reason.description: An optional justification supplied with the request.

requestedDurationSeconds.description: The time-bound (PIM/JIT) duration the requester proposes, in seconds; capped at approval to the deployment's maximum TTL. Absent means 'default to the maximum'.

grantedUntil.description: On approval, when the granted entitlement expires (now + min(requested, max-TTL)); absent for a standing grant. Echoes the binding's expiresAt for display/audit.

createdBy.description: The actor (the requester) that created the request.

decidedBy.description: The actor (an administrator, or the requester for a withdrawal) that decided the request, if decided.

decidedAt.description: When the request was decided, if decided.

decisionReason.description: An optional note recorded with the decision.

grantedBindingId.description: On approval, the id of the security-policy binding written to confer the grant (for audit and later revocation).
